focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Charlie, Ted's best friend and a butcher's son, uses beef analogies and is also a photographer, as Ted reflects in voiceover.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie is introduced using beef analogies and carrying a camera, establishing his confidence and close relationship with Ted.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie's identity as Ted's self-assured best friend who uses meat metaphors and practices photography is established.

relation_updates: established friendship with Ted
status_updates: uses beef analogies as personal quirk
auxiliary.persona_anchor: uses meat metaphors and camera as extensions of identity

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 7、INT. BASKETBALL COURT - TECUMSEH HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted defies Charlie's warnings and walks away, determined to reinvent himself, while introducing Julie Conroy, a new girl who already impacted his life.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie uses crude humor and voyeuristic photography to mock Ted's romantic hopes, emphasizing a worldview grounded in skepticism and sexual cynicism.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ted resolves to reinvent himself despite Charlie's warnings
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie remains dismissive of Ted's aspirations, reinforcing his role as a skeptic as Ted walks away determined to pursue change.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Charlie's warnings are ignored, leaving tension in their friendship due to clashing perspectives on possibility and self-transformation.
evidence_quotes: Charlie kneels by the sidelines. Shooting cheerleaders with his Nikon and an obscenely long lens.
Charlie's motor drive whirls. RAPID STILL SHOTS OF THE CHEERLEADER'S BOTTOM
CHARLIE That vibe is from your wrist, pal. You've been over-tenderizing your meat, again.
CHARLIE Don't do it, Ted. Don't do it.

relation_updates: tension emerges in friendship due to conflicting worldviews
auxiliary.persona_anchor: uses lewd humor to assert social realism

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Charlie mock each other's secrets, with Charlie revealing everyone knows Ted is a virgin, causing embarrassment as people stare; Ted considers reinventing their friendship.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie reveals Ted's virginity in a public setting, leading to Ted's visible embarrassment and a moment of distancing in their friendship.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted expresses a desire to find a new best friend after being publicly humiliated by Charlie.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Ted and Charlie's friendship can recover after the breach of trust and public shaming.
evidence_quotes: CHARLIE (CONT'D) ...EVERYBODY KNOWS YOU'RE A VIRGIN!
People stare at Ted as they pass.
TED I think I'll reinvent a new best friend.

relation_updates: friendship becomes strained
power dynamic shifts with public exposure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: prioritizes humor over loyalty in social moments

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Charlie in a limo; Charlie celebrates extravagantly with Dom Perignon, popping the cork, opening the sunroof, spilling champagne, while Ted remains anxious and out of place, noticing the Southern California sky.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: highlight of class contrast and coping styles
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie actively engages with the limo's luxuries, opening champagne and playing music, while disregarding Ted's concerns, highlighting his embrace of temporary privilege.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The difference in how Charlie and Ted respond to sudden luxury becomes evident, with Charlie reveling in the experience and Ted remaining tense and cautious.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Ted can relax and accept the new situation or will continue to resist it
evidence_quotes: Charlie continues playing with the console switches.
CHARLIE Don't you know anything about limo etiquette? Everything in here is included for our pleasure.
The cork flies through the air. Bounces off the ceiling. And hits another switch. The sun roof glides open.
Champagne overflows from the bottle. All over Charlie's crotch.

relation_updates: Charlie mocks Ted's caution
Ted resists Charlie's impulsivity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: treats excess as a moment to fully exploit

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Helen McDole, Ted's father's executive secretary, greets Ted emotionally, mistaking him for his father, while Charlie makes inappropriate remarks, prompting Ted to threaten sending him home.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie makes inappropriate remarks about Ted's inability to spit, prompting Ted to threaten sending him home.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain comic relief through boundary-pushing behavior
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie's behavior is met with a direct warning from Ted, making his continued presence conditional.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of being sent home
evidence_quotes: CHARLIE He never spits, ma'am. Ever. He can't even get a good hocker going. I've tried to teach him. It comes from the diaphragm.
TED (sharply; to Charlie) You're on the next plane home.

relation_updates: strained by Ted's threat to send him home
auxiliary.persona_anchor: tests social boundaries for comedic effect

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, entranced by Ginger, believes they died in a plane crash and are in heaven, with Charlie humorously imagining a room of chocolate, while Ted envisions fast cars.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie directs Ted's attention to Ginger emerging from the pool, prompting Ted to interpret the moment as evidence they are in heaven.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted becomes entranced and concludes that he and Charlie have died in a plane crash and are now in heaven.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ted's grasp on reality becomes uncertain as he conflates fantasy with actual events.
evidence_quotes: CHARLIE Check it out!
Charlie shoves the binoculars at Ted and guides him toward the window which overlooks the outdoor pool.
TED I get it now. You and me. We're dead.

relation_updates: Charlie reinforces role as instigator of discovery
status_updates: Ted enters a state of romantic awe
auxiliary.persona_anchor: acts as a trigger for Ted's transformative illusions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Charlie rip a pair of red silk panties while hiding from Ted's English butler, who departs discreetly, leaving them to bicker over the incident.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie handles and inhales Ted's mother's red silk panties, prompting a physical struggle with Ted that results in the panties being torn.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted and Charlie are left in awkward tension after being caught in the act by the butler, with the destroyed panties symbolizing their crossed boundary.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Charlie's disregard for personal boundaries conflicts with Ted's sense of respect and decorum.
evidence_quotes: Charlie pulls open a dresser drawer filled with Ted's mother's lingerie.
Charlie caresses a pair of red silk panties and inhales their powdery fragrance.
Ted grabs the panties just as Charlie pulls away, ripping them in half.
We were just--

relation_updates: tension between Charlie and Ted increases over boundaries
status_updates: Charlie crosses a line of decorum in the mansion
auxiliary.persona_anchor: prioritizes impulse over respect in sacred spaces

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, embraced as the 'Sovereign of Sexuality,' navigates his coming-out party with confidence, dancing and mingling with admirers, despite being inexperienced and improvising his way through attention and affection.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie actively joins the celebration at Ted's coming-out party, dancing enthusiastically and expressing pride in Ted's transformation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie is visibly engaged in the party, celebrating alongside Ted and participating in the social environment.
evidence_quotes: Charlie thinks he's Travolta. Dressed for disco. Twirling a Super Model.
CHARLIE Ain't life amazing? A week ago, you couldn't get to second base! Now you OWN second base! They high-five one another.

relation_updates: strengthens camaraderie with Ted through public affirmation
status_updates: actively participates in elite social scene
auxiliary.persona_anchor: takes joy in Ted's success and social ascent

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Merkin interrupts Charlie and Judy, asserting control; Judy resists, and as he drags her away, their flirtation continues. Flyers float down, attracting a group of women below.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie continues flirting with Judy after her father interrupts and physically intervenes, maintaining his composure and charm.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: pursuing romantic or flirtatious connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie remains composed and reiterates his flirtation after being disrupted by Principal Merkin.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential conflict with Principal Merkin due to continued interaction with his daughter
evidence_quotes: You're like a centerfold waiting to happen. And let me assure you, I have some serious pull with the publisher.
He grabs her arm and knocks the stack of flyers out of Charlie's hands.
Judy smiles back at Charlie,
Send us a picture, sugar. We'll get back to you.

relation_updates: begins flirtatious interaction with Judy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: unfazed by authority in pursuit of attraction

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, heartbroken over Ginger, is consoled by Charlie who points out the Taylor Triplets; Ted cheers up, declares the prom lame, and invites everyone to his place, leading a mass exodus as a new song plays.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie is seen openly kissing one of the Taylor Triplets while the others wait, and later challenges Principal Merkin to allow the prom to continue, reinforcing his role as a rule-defying presence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie remains unapologetically engaged with the Taylor Triplets and participates in the group's defiance of authority, contributing to the prom's disruption.
evidence_quotes: Here's the deal, Merk. Forget the "rules and regulations" malarkey and we'll give you seven minutes in heaven with the Taylor Triplets.

relation_updates: reinforces bond with Ted through shared rebellion
maintains ongoing romantic arrangement with Taylor Triplets
status_updates: established as a sexually confident rule-breaker
auxiliary.persona_anchor: lives by his own sexual and social code

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie receives a magazine from Charlie with a heartfelt dedication. She discovers photos of Principal Merkin in drag, performing as Barbra Streisand, alongside suggestive images with Molly, revealing his secret nightlife persona.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie delivers the magazine 'Heaven' to Julie and directs her to a dedicated message, sharing in the moment of revealing Principal Merkin's secret nightlife activities.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to share the success of the magazine and affirm connection with Julie
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie is visibly moved by the dedication and reacts with emotional and ironic appreciation to the exposé's content; Charlie stands by as a proud co-conspirator.
evidence_quotes: Charlie grins.
He hands her a copy of "Heaven."
CHARLIE Page three.
CHARLIE He kicked major ass and you know it.

relation_updates: reinforces close, affectionate relationship with Julie
status_updates: positioned as a key participant in the magazine's impact
auxiliary.persona_anchor: acts as a bridge between rebellion and emotional resolution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Charlie married the identical Taylor Triplets, had numerous children including multiple Charlie Jr.s, and Uncle Billy also found success.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlie marries all three Identical Taylor Triplets and becomes the father of numerous children, including multiple Charlie Jr.s, establishing an unconventional family unit.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlie lives on a farm with his three wives and many children, engaged in a chaotic but active family life.
evidence_quotes: Charlie wrestling in a haystack with the IDENTICAL TAYLOR TRIPLETS.
...where he married all three of the identical Taylor Triplets.
Peeking out of the haystack, we spot at least a dozen CHARLIE JR's.

relation_updates: married to all three Identical Taylor Triplets
status_updates: father of at least a dozen children
head of a large, unconventional family
auxiliary.persona_anchor: transforms youthful excess into lasting, absurd domesticity

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Charlie evolves from Ted's irreverent best friend and comic foil in high school to a loyal, if boundary-pushing, companion through Ted's sudden rise to wealth and identity crisis. His crude humor, voyeuristic photography, and sexual bravado contrast with Ted's growing emotional depth, yet he remains a consistent presence through key transformations. Ultimately, Charlie settles into an exaggerated version of his hedonistic persona, marrying the Taylor Triplets and fathering a large family, symbolizing both the fulfillment and absurdity of his youthful impulses.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 7、INT. BASKETBALL COURT - TECUMSEH HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted defies Charlie's warnings and walks away, determined to reinvent himself, while introducing Julie Conroy, a new girl who already impacted his life.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie Conroy is introduced as a new student observing Ted with concern during his defiant moment, marked by a freeze-frame that emphasizes her narrative significance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie is positioned as a figure of quiet importance in Ted's story, though her specific influence remains undefined.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the nature and extent of her prior impact on Ted's life is not explained
evidence_quotes: A quietly attractive teenaged girl, JULIE CONROY, watches Ted. She seems concerned, too.
FREEZE FRAME ON JULIE.

relation_updates: emerging connection with Ted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: observant, quietly influential newcomer

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 8、INT. TEACHER'S LOUNGE - TECUMSEH HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie emerges from an air vent, caught on camera by Principal Merkin, who realizes she's been exposing staff secrets, escalating tension after Miss Pratt's confession.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie is discovered emerging from an air vent holding a digital camera, revealing her as the creator of the 'EXPOSED!' pamphlets that document staff hypocrisy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: exposing institutional secrets
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie is publicly identified as the source of the 'EXPOSED!' campaign and becomes the focus of institutional confrontation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the consequences of her actions and potential disciplinary measures remain pending
evidence_quotes: THE WHOLE ROOM, IN SHOCKED SILENCE, turns to look at... JULIE, her head framed by the air duct.
She holds a digital camera in her hand.
Principal Merkin narrows his eyes at the "little bastard" who has been making his life hell.

relation_updates: adversarial relationship with Principal Merkin
auxiliary.persona_anchor: truth-seeking rebel with journalistic intent

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, pressured by a triple-dog-dare, publicly asks Christy to prom, causing mass laughter; she rejects him harshly, calling him pathetic, while Julie helps him up, calling it an emergency.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie intervenes after Ted's public humiliation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie helps Ted up after his failed prom proposal and calls the situation an 'actual emergency,' signaling her shift from sarcasm to direct support.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: supporting Ted through failure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie becomes a visible source of support for Ted immediately after his public rejection.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether her support is genuine or part of a larger agenda
evidence_quotes: Julie approaches. Gives him a hand. Helps him up.
JULIE No, Ted. This is an actual emergency.

relation_updates: shift from sarcasm to direct support
solidified bond with Ted
status_updates: now working on school paper under punishment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: loyal ally in moments of vulnerability

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Charlie bask in newfound popularity after their helicopter arrival; Ted flirts with Julie, who rebuffs his arrogance, while Charlie boasts insensitively, deepening her disdain.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie confronts Charlie and Ted's changed behavior with sarcasm, rejecting their new status and mocking their appearance and attitudes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resist superficial status shifts
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie distances herself from Ted and Charlie, expressing disapproval of their arrogance and self-promotion.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to respond to sudden social power shifts
evidence_quotes: JULIE What gives, Don Corleone?
JULIE New Hushpuppies don't make a new man.
JULIE That explains the emptiness that haunts me.
JULIE Second only to unfettered arrogance and the inability to see the irony therein.

relation_updates: increased disdain toward Ted
open hostility toward Charlie
auxiliary.persona_anchor: moral skeptic resisting performative success

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie confronts Ted about his newfound power and wealth, mocking his lack of change; she threatens to write a story about him, which intrigues him despite their tension.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie responds to Ted's criticism by threatening to write a story about his inheritance, challenging his authority and asserting her independence as a journalist.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain independence and professional credibility
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie moves from sarcastic defiance to making a concrete threat against Ted's public image.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether her job security depends on compliance
evidence_quotes: A few billion bucks and suddenly he's Cro-Magnon Man of the Year.
What is it, William Randolph? Your balls need scratching?
Then I'll write about YOU.

relation_updates: conflict with Ted intensifies
power dynamic tested
auxiliary.persona_anchor: principled journalist unafraid of power

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted offers financial freedom to his parents, urging them to pursue dreams and abandon their store, but they value their simple, honest life; Julie is moved, while Ted is disappointed by their refusal.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie is visibly moved by Ted's parents' commitment to their simple, honest life, which contrasts with Ted's emphasis on wealth and status.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie emotionally aligns with the values of hard work and authenticity demonstrated by Ted's parents.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between wealth and authenticity
evidence_quotes: Julie listens closely, admiring Ted's parents.
Tears fill her eyes. Mom and Dad moved her.

relation_updates: admiration for Ted's parents
emotional distance from Ted's worldview
auxiliary.persona_anchor: value-driven observer of authenticity

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Drunken teens leave a prom; Ted is intercepted by Julie on a Harley, who offers him a ride, and he joins her, holding onto her waist as she drives off.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie physically intercepts Ted on her motorcycle and offers him a ride, transitioning from observer to direct participant in his night.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: connect with Ted beyond the lens, on her own terms
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie becomes directly involved in Ted's post-prom experience through physical proximity and shared movement.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the nature and risk of her growing emotional investment
evidence_quotes: Suddenly he's cut off by a Harley that stops right in front of him.
JULIE Need a lift?
Ted jumps on, wrapping his arms around her waist.
Julie takes off.

relation_updates: physical closeness established through motorcycle ride
shifts from professional distance to personal involvement
status_updates: in control of movement and direction (driving the Harley)
auxiliary.persona_anchor: agent of connection through action

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Principal Merkin gives soup to a homeless man while Julie gives Ted a knowing look, implying she had previously predicted or advised the action.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: validating prior judgment and shifting power in relationship
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie gives Ted an 'I told you so' look when Merkin helps a homeless man, signaling that she had anticipated or approved of the action while Ted had doubted it.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie feels confirmed in her positive assessment of Merkin and gains confidence relative to Ted's skepticism.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Merkin's act of helping the homeless man reflects genuine goodness or is performative.
evidence_quotes: Julie shoots Ted an "I told you so" look.

relation_updates: gains upper hand in dynamic with Ted
status_updates: proves her assessment of Merk was correct
auxiliary.persona_anchor: morally consistent and perceptive

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Julie, spying on their principal at a nightclub, witness him performing in drag as a torch singer, capturing the moment as he remains unaware his secret is exposed.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional and relational turning point during shared mission
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie states that trust is the most important thing in relationships and explicitly refuses to trust Ted in the moment, though she leaves open the possibility for the future.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: balancing personal boundaries with growing feelings for Ted
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie remains emotionally cautious, resisting immediate romantic escalation while acknowledging potential for future connection.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether she can come to trust Ted fully
evidence_quotes: I realized the most important thing to me, above all, is trust.
You lose trust in someone and it's over, done, gone, forever.
Do you trust me, Julie?
Not a chance.

relation_updates: resists Ted's intimacy but acknowledges potential for future trust
auxiliary.persona_anchor: emotionally guarded but open to growth

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 84、INT. TECUMSEH HIGH SCHOOL - GIRL'S LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Christy's intimate moment is interrupted by Julie, who catches them in the act, reacts with hurt, and leaves as Ted chases after her while Christy covers herself.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie discovers Ted in a physical encounter with Christy, responds with sarcasm, and leaves without hearing an explanation, marking a clear break in trust.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie ends any romantic possibility with Ted by walking away without engaging further.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the relationship can recover from betrayal
evidence_quotes: Suddenly, they roll into a pair of legs.
JULIE What's up, doc?
She doesn't wait for an explanation.
Ted chases after Julie.

relation_updates: trust in Ted is broken after catching him with Christy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: wounded but dignified in rejection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie asserts nudity lacks emotional meaning, criticizes Ted's magazine, and leaves as his phone rings with news of reader backlash, leaving him isolated and defeated.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie undresses in front of Ted to demonstrate that nudity is physically exposed but emotionally meaningless, rejects his justification for kissing another girl, and criticizes his magazine as lacking depth, marking a clear emotional separation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assert emotional integrity and reject Ted's objectification
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie disengages from Ted with composure and finality, leaving him alone and emotionally defeated.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ted remains emotionally attached to Julie while she withdraws completely
evidence_quotes: JULIE Go away, Ted. Just go.
She unzips the back of her summery dress. And lets it fall to the floor. She's completely naked and absolutely gorgeous.
She puts on her dress, gracefully. As if nothing happened.

relation_updates: emotional distance from Ted solidified
moral judgment of Ted's values expressed
status_updates: takes control of the interaction through vulnerability on her own terms
auxiliary.persona_anchor: self-possessed and morally grounded

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted graduates summa cum laude, launches 'EXPOSED!' magazine honoring Julie, who becomes editor; they dance to their song, celebrating their love as friends cheer, ending on a joyful note.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie reconciles with Ted after his graduation, accepts his declaration of love, and agrees to join his new magazine as editor-in-chief.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to be loved and valued for who she is, not objectified
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: reunited with Ted in a committed relationship and professionally appointed as editor-in-chief of 'EXPOSED!'.
evidence_quotes: JULIE I'm not going anywhere, Ted. You're the one with the champagne wishes and caviar dreams.
JULIE I know where you can find an excellent editor-in-chief.

relation_updates: reunited with Ted
romantic commitment confirmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: empowered partner and professional equal

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Julie Conroy enters Ted's world as a quiet observer but quickly emerges as a moral and journalistic force, challenging hypocrisy and materialism. Her relationship with Ted evolves from skeptical detachment to mutual attraction, punctuated by conflict over values, betrayal, and eventual reconciliation. She remains grounded in integrity throughout, ultimately becoming both Ted’s emotional anchor and professional partner. The arc traces her journey from rebellious outsider to empowered insider, committed to truth and authentic connection.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A teenage boy rides to Tecumseh High School as a reflective voiceover muses on sudden life-changing moments and the universal possibility of dreams coming true.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted narrates his belief that life can change suddenly and dramatically, positioning himself as an ordinary teen hoping for a transformative opportunity.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: desire to become somebody significant
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: remains an unnamed student arriving at school, hopeful but still in a state of anticipation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about his future identity and how or if his life will change
evidence_quotes: On any given day, your life can change in the blink of an eye.
one day you're a regular chump, a wannabe something.
The next day the doorbell rings and some stranger's holding your ticket to being SOMEBODY.
It could happen to any guy, any time...even ME.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: self-aware teen with a hopeful, aspirational outlook

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Christy Malone hides the scandalous 'Exposed!' newspaper as Principal Merkin confiscates copies; Ted interprets her wink as a romantic signal and decides to ask her to prom.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted interprets Christy Malone's wink as a romantic invitation and decides to ask her to prom.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to pursue Christy Malone romantically
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: moves from passive admiration to deciding to take action by asking Christy to prom
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about whether Christy's wink was genuinely romantic or merely playful
evidence_quotes: TED (V.O.) That's Christy Malone. Head cheerleader, love of my life, lust of my loins.
Christy calls to Ted.... CHRISTY (CONT'D) Look at this, Ted!
Christy winks at Ted as she hides the newspaper in her locker.

relation_updates: perceives growing intimacy with Christy Malone
auxiliary.persona_anchor: idealistic romantic seeking validation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, pressured by a triple-dog-dare, publicly asks Christy to prom, causing mass laughter; she rejects him harshly, calling him pathetic, while Julie helps him up, calling it an emergency.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted is publicly rejected by Christy after being dared to ask her to prom, and Julie helps him recover from the embarrassment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to win Christy's affection through bold action
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted is left humiliated and isolated after Christy's harsh rejection, while Julie offers him support, marking the start of a deeper connection.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Ted will shift his focus from unattainable romantic ideals to meaningful relationships
evidence_quotes: Christy looks mortified. CHRISTY (through gritted teeth) Ted, have you lost your mind?
CHRISTY I don't LIKE you. We're JUST FRIENDS.
CHRISTY The answer is NEVER, Ted, NEVER. Except maybe in your dreams.
Julie approaches. Gives him a hand. Helps him up. JULIE No, Ted. This is an actual emergency.

relation_updates: romantic pursuit of Christy definitively rejected
bond with Julie solidified through support in crisis
status_updates: social status damaged by public ridicule
auxiliary.persona_anchor: vulnerable youth learning emotional resilience

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marty Zukerman tells Ted his biological parents died in the Amazon from a piranha attack, but Ted initially believes it's a mistake, thinking of his adoptive parents.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted is informed by Marty Zukerman that his biological parents died in a piranha attack in the Amazon, contradicting his assumptions about his family history.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: grappling with new identity as adopted child
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: shocked and disoriented after learning the truth about his biological parents' death
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about his biological roots and how this new information affects his sense of self
evidence_quotes: TED You've got the wrong guy. The closest my parents have been to the Amazon is the Brazilian pavilion at Epcot.
ZUKERMAN Well kid, I'm speaking of...your biological parents.

relation_updates: initially hostile, then intrigued interaction with Marty Zukerman
auxiliary.persona_anchor: identity in flux due to familial revelation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted confirms liking girls; Uncle Billy seeks reassurance about his nephew's interests, relieved by Charlie's humorous clarification that Ted is enthusiastically attracted to women.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted learns he has inherited the 'Heaven' empire and that his biological parents, Heather and Tom Deeds, founded it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: process sudden inheritance and family revelation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted transitions from an ordinary teenager to the legal owner of a national media and entertainment empire founded by his late biological parents.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: struggles to reconcile his personal identity with the legacy of his parents and the nature of their business.
evidence_quotes: ZUKERMAN Your fathered owned the entire \"Heaven\" empire. You own it now. Any questions?
HEATHER But we did want you. It's just that you don't always get to keep what you want. We knew you needed a real mom and dad.
TOM We had no idea the nudie magazine we started in the garage would turn into all this.

relation_updates: learns Heather and Tom Deeds are his biological parents
status_updates: becomes owner of a national media empire
learns he is central to a legacy he never knew existed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: reluctant inheritor of a provocative legacy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted embraces his identity as heir to a billion-dollar empire, symbolized by buying the entire case of sunglasses, signaling his acceptance of a grand destiny.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted purchases an entire case of sunglasses after a conversation that challenges his self-identity, marking a shift in his attitude toward wealth and status.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: defining who Ted Nelson is
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted appears more confident in his new identity as someone entitled to great wealth and luxury.
evidence_quotes: Ted tries on some snazzy sunglasses.
TED Sorry, habit.
GINGER Who exactly ARE you?
TED I'll take the whole case.

relation_updates: Ginger challenges and shapes Ted's self-conception
status_updates: Ted embraces identity as heir to vast wealth
auxiliary.persona_anchor: performing confidence amid transformation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted offers financial freedom to his parents, urging them to pursue dreams and abandon their store, but they value their simple, honest life; Julie is moved, while Ted is disappointed by their refusal.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: wealthy son trying to improve his family's life
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted offers to financially support his parents so they can stop working, but they decline, expressing pride in their store and simple life; Ted reacts with disappointment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: enable his parents to stop working and enjoy life through his wealth
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: faces rejection of his financial help and confronts the mismatch between his values and his family's
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: difficulty reconciling his success with his family's refusal to change their lifestyle
evidence_quotes: TED You don't have to work another day in your lives.
DAD Ted, we put a lot of love in our business.
MOM We wanted you and Jimmy to have it someday. Something real and honest.
DAD We love our lives just the way they are.

relation_updates: strained relationship with parents due tomismatch in values
status_updates: realizes money cannot buy the life he imagined for others
auxiliary.persona_anchor: idealistic but disillusioned by his family's resistance to his vision of a better life

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 84、INT. TECUMSEH HIGH SCHOOL - GIRL'S LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Christy's intimate moment is interrupted by Julie, who catches them in the act, reacts with hurt, and leaves as Ted chases after her while Christy covers herself.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: partner caught in a breach of trust
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted attempts to stop an intimate encounter with Christy after recalling Julie's emphasis on trust, but is seen in a compromising position by Julie, who leaves in hurt silence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resist acting on attraction to Christy in order to honor his relationship with Julie
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted physically disengages from Christy after expressing hesitation, but is still found in a highly intimate situation by Julie, leading to her emotional departure
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Julie will accept Ted's attempt to explain and if their relationship can recover from the perceived betrayal
evidence_quotes: Remembering what Julie said about trust.
He turns away, but Christy presses her bare body against his back. Wrapping her hands around his waist.
The steam clears to reveal Julie. Arms crossed. Hurt, but not surprised.

relation_updates: romantic bond with Julie is broken by perceived betrayal
physical intimacy with Christy escalates then collapses
auxiliary.persona_anchor: struggles to align actions with stated values when faced with strong emotional temptation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, overwhelmed by his success, expresses regret over his fame and fortune, feeling grief instead of happiness, and considers abandoning his magazine until Helen offers comfort and guidance.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted expresses regret over his success, feeling grief rather than fulfillment, and considers abandoning his magazine.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to escape the pain of success and abandon the magazine
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally vulnerable, questioning his life choices
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether to continue leading 'Heaven'
evidence_quotes: I always thought I wanted fame and fortune and beautiful women. But sometimes your dreams come true, and they cause you nothing but grief.
I just want to chuck everything and sell the stupid magazine. It's not worth the pain.

relation_updates: Helen provides emotional support
auxiliary.persona_anchor: success without satisfaction

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted admits fault for changing the shelf layout, restores canned peaches to meet customer expectations, earns stockholder approval, and fires Uncle Billy, affirming family is based on love, not blood.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted admits he made a mistake changing the shelf layout, announces the return of canned peaches to meet customer expectations, gains stockholder support, and fires Uncle Billy while stating that family is based on love, not blood.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to regain trust and restore customer-focused operations
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: regains leadership control and publicly aligns company decisions with values from his adoptive father
evidence_quotes: TED (O.S.) --PEACHES! CANNED PEACHES!
Ted bursts into the meeting with his Dad. Holding up a can of peaches.
So, I ask you for one more chance to fix things and give our readers back their canned peaches.
The stockholders APPLAUD enthusiastically, giving Ted a standing ovation.

relation_updates: defies and fires Uncle Billy
honors adoptive father's values in public
status_updates: establishes leadership through humility and authenticity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: leader who prioritizes humility and customer trust

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted graduates summa cum laude, launches 'EXPOSED!' magazine honoring Julie, who becomes editor; they dance to their song, celebrating their love as friends cheer, ending on a joyful note.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted expresses regret over losing Julie, announces plans to step down from running the current magazine, commit to college, and launch a new magazine as a tribute to her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: pursuing education, authentic love, and purposeful work
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: reunited with Julie and publicly reaffirming his commitment to a more meaningful path forward
evidence_quotes: TED I decided to let Helen run the magazine. I need to go to college, learn a few things, you know.

relation_updates: relationship with Julie restored and deepened through mutual affection
status_updates: commits to stepping down from current magazine leadership
plans to launch 'EXPOSED!' as a tribute to Julie
auxiliary.persona_anchor: grounded idealist embracing authentic purpose

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted and Julie sail into a glorious sunset as the scene fades out.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted sails into the sunset with Julie, marking the start of a shared journey.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: on a sailboat with Julie, traveling along the Pacific Coast
evidence_quotes: Ted and Julie sail off into, what else but a GLORIOUS SUNSET.

relation_updates: shared journey with Julie
status_updates: embarking on a new life phase
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted as a companion embarking on a new life phase

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Ted evolves from an ordinary, self-deprecating high school student in Xenia, Ohio, into the heir of a controversial media empire, confronting questions of identity, authenticity, and belonging. His journey traces a path from romantic delusion and social insecurity to sudden wealth and public influence, followed by disillusionment and a return to core values. Ultimately, Ted reconciles his biological legacy with his adoptive family's love, rejects superficial power, and commits to integrity, education, and a genuine relationship with Julie. The arc culminates in personal maturity, emotional accountability, and a future defined by purpose rather than spectacle.
